allowed_uses: record public DCA bot configuration concepts and required parameter names
design canonical DCA reconstruction candidates for frozen-data testing
compare only local offline results after validation gates are complete
bias_risks: product backtest claims may use unavailable assumptions or data
platform exchange integrations are credential-bearing and unavailable in S2
DCA variants require explicit sizing, safety-order, and exit semantics
notes: No 3Commas account, exchange link, trial signup, or bot execution is allowed in S2.
This plan captures configuration hypotheses only.

backtest_claim_visibility.value: Public product surface advertises historical-data backtesting and strategy refinement, but local validation evidence is not inherited.

credential_or_account_controls_present.value: Platform operation depends on linked exchange accounts; S2 capture remains metadata-only with no account or API-key path.

exchange_dependency_visibility.value: Public product page states integration with multiple exchanges such as Binance, Bybit, OKX, Coinbase Advanced, KuCoin, and Kraken.

visible_parameters.value: Public product/help surfaces describe naming a bot, selecting an exchange, choosing bot type, selecting trading pairs, and configuring DCA settings.

validation_prerequisites: local replay with declared cost grid
independent validation package before any promotion claim
